'''Jamal Fakir''' (born 30 August 1982) is a Moroccan [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} or {{rlp|sr}} for the [[Lézignan Sangliers]] club in the [[Elite One Championship]].

==Background==
Fakir was born in [[Fez, Morocco]]. He is [[Morocco|Moroccan]]-French.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2006/mar/21/rugbyleague.sport1|work=The Guardian|location=London|title=Scouting report: Jamal Fakir, Catalans Dragons|first=John|last=Kear|date=21 March 2006|access-date=2010-05-05}}</ref>

==Career==
Fakir caught the attention of [[Super League]] and French [[rugby union]] sides before the Dragons' entry into Super League in 2006. After a strong debut season in Super League, Fakir's progress has been hampered by two serious knee injuries, firstly a ruptured patella tendon sustained in late 2006, and then cruciate ligament damage sustained in May 2007 which ended his season.

Fakir was named in the [[France national rugby league team#2008 World Cup Squad|France training squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="France dominated by Dragons">{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12196_3949078,00.html|title=France dominated by Dragons|publisher=Sky Sports|date=7 August 2008|access-date=2008-08-08}}</ref> He was named in the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#France|France squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rlwc08.com/news/news/archive/2008/09/24/france-name-official-squad.aspx |title=France name official squad |publisher=RLWC2008 |date=24 September 2008 |access-date=2008-10-03 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080930072635/http://www.rlwc08.com/news/news/archive/2008/09/24/france-name-official-squad.aspx |archive-date=30 September 2008 }}</ref>

Fakir became a Moroccan international in 2009.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.rugby-league-world.com/2000/WorldCup/emergingnations/squads.htm |title=Emerging nations. Squad |access-date=31 July 2009 |archive-date=4 December 2008 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20081204181112/http://www.rugby-league-world.com/2000/WorldCup/emergingnations/squads.htm |url-status=dead }}</ref>

He agreed to switch codes in May 2010 to play [[rugby union]], and intended to join [[Castres Olympique]] on a one-year deal at the beginning of the 2010/2011 season.<ref>[http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/news/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=rleague/10/05/14/RUGBYL_Catalans.html Rugby league] Sporting Life {{Dead link|date=February 2022}}</ref> In addition to his deal with Castres he also had interest from Scottish club [[Glasgow Warriors]].<ref>{{cite news|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/catalan/8683144.stm|work=BBC News|title=Fakir opts to stay with Catalans|date=1 August 2010}}</ref> Fakir however reversed his decision and decided to stay with the Catalans for a further three seasons.

Capped for France against [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] in the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]] group play round 2, coming off the bench to make an impact in the loss.

On 3 April 2014, Fakir signed for [[Super League]] team [[Bradford Bulls]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/article/29888/bulls-land-fakir |title=Bulls land Fakir |access-date=3 April 2014 |archive-date=7 April 2014 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140407064143/http://www.superleague.co.uk/article/29888/bulls-land-fakir |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/0/rugby-league/26869517|title = Bradford Bulls sign prop Fakir|work = BBC Sport}}</ref>

He played in the [[2014 European Cup]].
